purpose: Screen EM currencies for ex ante vulnerability to depreciation during DM tightening cycles using balance-sheet and flow indicators.
components: Current account deficit as percentage of GDP (contributes to twin deficit threshold of >5% combined)
Fiscal deficit as percentage of GDP (contributes to twin deficit threshold)
Short-term external debt to reserves ratio (high ratio indicates rollover vulnerability)
Reserve adequacy in months of import cover (current EM median ~7 months vs ~5 in 2013)
Complementary exporter/importer filter to capture terms-of-trade channel alongside balance-sheet channel
Twin-deficit threshold: current account deficit + fiscal deficit exceeding 5% of GDP
Push vs pull classification: filter applies to push (DM-driven) episodes only
Hit rate: 70-80% for >20% depreciation when filter triggered during push episodes
Frontier-to-core cascade monitoring: 3-6 month transmission lag from frontier to core EM
Current vulnerability map: Egypt, Pakistan as frontier; Brazil, Mexico as core EM to monitor
current_assessment: Current environment maps to push scenario (DM tightening, broad dollar strength). Monitoring whether frontier weakness cascades to core EM is the key signpost for severity assessment.
limitations: 70-80% hit rate means 20-30% false positive rate
Thresholds may need adjustment for countries with large sovereign wealth funds
Does not capture political and institutional risk (capital controls, central bank independence)
Historical sample is dominated by a few large episodes and may not capture novel stress channels
Does not differentiate within the vulnerable set which currencies will be most affected
Pull episodes require different analytical framework
Reserve adequacy and external debt maturity may improve discrimination but are not yet incorporated
